changelog.change: Mar 15 autopsy — 4 structural fixes: (1) chalk_min_boost_floor=1.2 (new) — minimum card boost for Starting 5 eligibility. Westbrook (1.1x override) and Clingan (1.0x override) won 2.0x/1.8x chalk slots despite near-zero boost, killing Starting 5 EV. Stars below 1.2x boost are now excluded from chalk pool entirely. (2) Wildcard gate relaxed: wildcard_min_minutes 24→10, wildcard_min_boost 2.9→2.5, wildcard_min_season_pts 8→5. GPII (predMin 16.5, boost 2.7x) and similar deep-bench high-boost plays were blocked by 24-min gate; now surfaced as moonshot wildcards. (3) dev_team_pts_floor 10→6 — dev team win_pct rebuild bonus was suppressed for players scoring <10ppg. Williams (6ppg UTAH) and Cardwell (5.4ppg SAC) now receive the bonus, improving moonshot slot ranking for low-scoring dev-team role players. (4) is_role_spike path added to moonshot eligibility — players with recent_min ≥ 25 AND recent/season ratio ≥ 1.4 now qualify regardless of season_min floor. Catches Hayes (season 16min, recent 26min, 2.7x boost, actual RS 3.3 / value 16.5) and similar role-change pickups. dnp_risk_min_threshold 8→5: stopped flagging 5-8min players as inactive, giving deep bench contrarians a chance to pass projection.
Initial config externalization
March 4 audit: raise min_gate 12→15, add GTD penalty (0.75x), DNP risk filter (< 8 recent min = skip), reliability multiplier on chalk_ev (penalizes minute-inconsistent + GTD players)
Model improvements: min_gate 15→12 (boost-aware gate in code), SA removed from big_market_teams, star_players list added for per-player ownership override

March 5 audit: added development_teams list (UTA/IND/BKN/CHI/NOP/SAC/MEM/WAS/DAL), moonshot overhaul (20-min floor, minutes*boost ranking, RotoWire lineup clearance), RotoWire scraper integration for lineup confirmations

March 6 audit (Mar 5 results): chalk_boost_cap 1.0→1.5 (Bailey 187 drafts RS 5.9 +2.1x beat Wemby 1.4k drafts RS 7.1 +0.3x — cap was preventing high-boost role players from chalk); RotoWire check added to chalk lineup (fixes OUT/questionable players appearing in Starting 5)

Production hardening: added lineup.{chalk_rating_floor=2.8, game_chalk_rating_floor=3.5, avg_slot_multiplier=1.6, slot_multipliers}, line.{min_confidence=50, min_edge_pct=3.0}, moonshot.min_rating_floor=2.0. All lineup/line floors now tunable via Ben without code deploys. Added _normalize_player/_normalize_line_pick contract layer in api/index.py to decouple model internals from frontend.

March 6 results audit — 5 fixes: (1) RS compression recalibrated — divisor 5→7, power 0.75→0.62 (Jokic pred 11.7 vs actual 5.6, Wemby 10.4 vs 4.5); (2) Log card boost formula ACTIVATED — fitted to Real Sports actual data: boost=3.76-0.88*log10(drafts). New star_hype_multiplier=15x separates stars (9k drafts→0.2 boost) from role players (13 drafts→3.0 boost). scalar=50 with big_market 1.5x + star 15x stacking; (3) chalk_boost_cap 1.5→2.5 — all 3 winning lineups stacked +3.0x boost in Starting 5; (4) chalk_rating_floor 2.8→2.0 — Ighodaro RS 2.3 in ALL 3 winning drafts; (5) star_players expanded: +LaMelo, Kawhi, Jaylen Brown, Knueppel, Sheppard, Clingan (high-ownership players model should penalize).

Line of the Day: projections enriched with real last-5 game stats (nba_api) before engine run so over/under picks use actual recent form. Line section de-hardcoded: recent_form_over_ratio (1.08), recent_form_under_ratio (0.92), min_edge_pts (2.0), min_edge_other (1.5) now tunable via Ben.

Mar 8 screenshot audit (10-game Sunday slate): (1) star_players expanded: +Austin Reaves (160 drafts → +0.5x actual; LAL brand drives outsized ownership vs RS output), +Jalen Brunson (3.2k drafts → +0.4x; NYK franchise player model was massively underestimating), +Scottie Barnes (2.2k drafts → +0.5x; TOR franchise star), +RJ Barrett (9 drafts → +1.0x; star-penalized by app despite low drafts). (2) cascade.per_player_cap_minutes 3.0 → 2.0: Sunday moonshot cascade picks (Schroeder +3.0 min → RS 0.4, Robinson +3.0 → RS 1.0, Chapel +3.0 → RS 0.8) all missed badly — 3 min bonus was too aggressive. (3) development_teams: added POR (Scoot Henderson — 4 drafts, RS 5.3, +2.5x — top value Sunday but not in model's moonshot universe). (4) card_boost.pool_size_estimate=10000 added for future calibrate-boost endpoint.

Minutes floor tuning (season avg): (1) chalk_season_min_floor 20→22 (season avg) — Caruso (18.5 avg) is a moonshot-profile player (high card boost, low season role) not a Starting 5 pick; using season avg as the stable baseline keeps inflated recent splits from promoting low-role players. Configurable via projection.chalk_season_min_floor. (2) moonshot.min_minutes_floor 20→18 (season avg) — catches borderline role players averaging 18-20 min who were excluded by the old 20-min floor. Both floors now use season_min instead of recent_min for stability.

Sync stale/missing config values that code-default changes never propagated: projection.chalk_season_min_floor 22→30; projection.chalk_recent_min_floor added (15.0); moonshot.min_minutes_floor 18→25; moonshot.card_boost_weight 2.0→2.5; moonshot.min_recent_minutes_floor added (22); moonshot.big_pos_efficiency added (0.65); line.min_season_minutes added (20.0)

Moonshot minutes filter: raise min_recent_minutes_floor 22→25 to exclude recently-benched backups. Ensures all moonshot picks have ≥25 min/game average to filter out spot-use players like Capela (12.3), Robinson (19.3), DeAndre (18.3) who don't have consistent rotation roles.

Moonshot EV overhaul (targeting 70+ total): (1) Filter changed from OR to AND — hard recent_min ≥25 + season_min ≥20 (no more backup centers via inflated recent splits). (2) Center cap: max 2 centers in moonshot — centers generate fewer RS events (screens/rim-protection). Winners use guards/forwards from dev teams. (3) big_pos_efficiency 0.65→0.40 — much stronger center penalty in ranking. (4) Added ORL to development_teams — da Silva, Clifford appear in winning leaderboards consistently. (5) Decline factor softened — trigger 0.90→0.80, capped at 0.85 (max 15% penalty). Thompson ↓ 17% was over-penalized.

Mar 14 Saturday autopsy (draft scored <50 vs optimal 83): 6 fixes. (1) Card boost recalibrated — log_a 3.76→4.2, log_b 0.88→1.1, ownership_scalar 50→80; steeper curve separates truly obscure (<100 drafts → 2.0+ boost) from mid-popularity (200+ drafts → <1.2x). Suggs 275 drafts was getting 1.6x predicted vs 1.1x actual. (2) Boost leverage power added to moonshot — boost_leverage_power=1.3 baked into adj_ceiling so MILP strongly favors high-boost players. 3.0x boost → 3.7x adj_ceiling weight vs 1.0x. Old formula gave only 10% advantage. (3) min_card_boost 1.0→1.5 — excludes mid-popularity players (Suggs, Jaquez 815 drafts) from moonshot entirely. (4) big_pos_efficiency 0.40→0.70 — centers CAN win moonshot (Queta RS 4.9, Achiuwa RS 5.1 were top value). 0.40x was too punishing. (5) Defensive rate bonus — STL+BLK per-36 above average gives up to 20% moonshot adj_ceiling bonus. Smart (1.4 STL) gets ~6% bonus; elite defenders get 15-20%. (6) big_market_teams trimmed — removed MIL, DAL, PHX (role players on these teams are NOT heavily drafted; only stars are).

Over model tightening (17% hit rate → targeting 55%+): (1) stat_floors.rebounds 2.0→5.5 — eliminates fringe rebounders (Nance 2.5, Love borderline) from rebounds picks; only legit rotation bigs qualify. (2) min_edge_other_over: 2.5 (new) — over picks on rebounds/assists need a 2.5+ projection edge vs the 1.5 threshold kept for under picks; rebounds are too volatile for small-edge overs. (3) recent_form_over_ratio 1.08→1.15 — require 15% recent form spike to unlock the +12 confidence bonus for overs (was 8%); under model unchanged at 0.92. (4) Claude prompt AVOID clause updated — rebounds/assists overs now require a catalyst (cascade, opp-B2B, or 230+ total); prefer points over when edge quality is similar.

Complete boost model rewrite — lookup + sigmoid tier (v23). Cross-date analysis proved boost is a STABLE per-player attribute (Wemby +0.3x on both 1400 and 8500 drafts, Cade +0.2x on both 5300 and 1200 drafts, Bam +0.6x on both 2200 and 388 drafts). Old formula tried to predict daily drafts from PPG/minutes — fundamentally wrong. New approach: (1) player_overrides lookup from 60+ real observations across Mar 6/9/10 ownership/actuals data — exact match returns known boost. (2) Sigmoid tier fallback for unknown players: boost = 3.0 - 2.8 × sigmoid((PPG - 12) / 4). Calibrated: 25+ PPG → ~0.2x, 20 PPG → ~0.5x, 15 PPG → ~1.1x, 12 PPG → ~1.6x, 10 PPG → ~1.9x, 8 PPG → ~2.3x, <5 PPG → ~2.6x. Big market discount -0.15. Removed: decay_base, scalar, base_offset, log_formula_active, log_a, log_b, log_ownership_scalar, fame_pts_base, fame_exponent, fame_cap.

14-date backtest (Feb 28 - Mar 14, 56 winning lineups): Gate C wildcard tier relaxed. (1) wildcard_min_minutes 15→8 — Santos/Clifford/Walker archetype (7-14 min avg) consistently appeared in winning lineups (Santos in 8/14 dates, Clifford in 5/14) but Gate C's 15-min floor excluded them even after v21 added the wildcard tier. 8-min floor matches the pipeline effective_gate floor so any player who passes projection also qualifies for wildcard evaluation. (2) wildcard_min_boost 2.5→2.0 — our boost estimation runs systematically 30-50% below actual leaderboard boosts (e.g. Clifford estimated ~2.3x, actual 4.2-4.8x; Raynaud estimated ~2.2x, actual 3.3-4.2x). Lowering the threshold ensures near-wildcard players qualify through Gate C when their estimated boost is in the 2.0-2.5 range.

Leaderboard autopsy Mar 11-14 (winners 69-82, our sim ~55-67): 4 fixes. (1) Moonshot wildcard gate C — ultra-high-boost bench players (boost>=2.5, pred_min>=15) now qualify regardless of season/recent min floor. Captures Santos/Plowden/Hendricks archetype (10-18 min avg, 3-6ppg, 4.0-4.5x total multiplier) that appeared in all 4 winning leaderboards but was outside our projection pool. Wildcards bypass the 2.0 rating floor (their upside is pure boost leverage). (2) Moonshot max_per_team 2→3 (configurable via moonshot.max_per_team) — March 14 optimal play was 3-5 SAC players (Achiuwa RS 5.1, Raynaud RS 3.8, Westbrook RS 4.1, Clifford RS 2.1, Plowden RS 3.3); hardcoded max_per_team=2 prevented this entire stack. (3) S5 leverage_top_slots 2→1 — winning lineups put the high-RS anchor (Bane 6.5, Doncic 9.3, Murray 6.7, Jokic 5.1-6.5) in the 2.0x TOP slot, but our leverage_top_slots=2 forced both top slots to contrarians, never allowing a star anchor. (4) Dev team pts floor (moonshot.dev_team_pts_floor=8.0) — dev team bonus now suppressed for players with pts<8 (aging bench fodder like Jordan 6pts on NO got 1.30x undeservedly; Sensabaugh 12pts/UTAH still gets full bonus).

Moonshot variance penalty + dev-team bench threshold: (1) moonshot.variance_penalty=0.3 — ceiling_score was rewarding RS variance (Westbrook gets +30% adj_ceiling boost from volatility), making boom/bust high-usage players outrank reliable role players. Replaced ceiling_score with consistency_base = raw_score × max(0.75, 1.0 - variance × 0.3). Westbrook-type (variance=0.6) loses 37% of moonshot ranking; Nesmith-type (variance=0.1) loses only 7%. (2) bench_pts_threshold 12→14, bench_min_threshold 26→30 — dev-team rotation players like P.J. Washington (11pts, 28min) and Marshall (~14pts, 28min) were incorrectly classified as 'stars' and got the spread penalty instead of the bench blowout bonus. Raising the thresholds captures role players on rebuilding teams who play extended minutes.

Big-man calibration: (1) Post-LightGBM multiplier for low-scoring interior bigs (projection.big_man_calibration) — LightGBM has no rebounding feature, causing systematic underestimation of C/PF players like Poeltl (9ppg, 9reb → was projected ~2.5 RS, actual 5.4) and Portis (14ppg, 9reb → actual 5.2). Multiplier applies to pos=C/F with season_pts < 16 only, capped at +40%. reb_bonus = (season_reb-6)×0.04, blk_bonus = season_blk×0.10. High-scoring stars (Giannis 28ppg, Randle 24ppg) untouched. (2) big_pos_efficiency 0.70→0.85 — empirical data shows ~15% efficiency gap vs guards, not 30%. 0.70 was over-penalizing exactly the bigs we want in moonshot.

Mar 15 moonshot blind spots: (1) min_recent_minutes_floor 25→20 — Caruso (~20 min, +3.0x actual) and Walker (~20-22 min, +2.3x actual) were blocked by the minutes gate before boost was even evaluated. The boost filter (min_card_boost=1.5) is the real quality gate; the minutes floor was over-conservative. (2) boost_leverage_power 1.3→1.6 — at 1.3, a +3.0x player had only 1.6x adj_ceiling advantage over a +2.0x player, not enough to overcome RS projection differences. At 1.6 the ratio is 1.91x, making ultra-low-ownership plays rank decisively higher.

Mar 15 ownership model audit: (1) fame_pts_base 8.0→14.0 — role players (10-14 ppg) were being treated as famous, inflating predicted ownership and suppressing boost estimates. Nesmith (12ppg) was getting ~1.46x estimated boost → filtered out by min_card_boost=1.5 despite +1.9x actual. Fix: only 18+ ppg scorers attract meaningful ownership inflation. (2) Replaced hardcoded development_teams list with continuous rebuild multiplier from live ESPN standings — team_bonus = max(1.0, 1.0 + max(0, 0.5 - win_pct)). SAC .250 → 1.25x, MEM .348 → 1.15x, competitive teams (.500+) → 1.0x. No maintenance required, auto-updates with standings daily. Removed development_teams and dev_team_boost from config entirely.

Starting 5 audit (Mar 14 leaderboard: top 6 all scored 69-71, our S5 scored ~45). 6/6 winners had Achiuwa, 5/6 Raynaud, 4/6 Westbrook — high-boost role players excluded by our 30-min floor. 4/6 winners had ZERO stars. Fixes: (1) chalk_season_min_floor 30→25 — Smart (28.9min), Achiuwa (~26min) were excluded; this was THE bottleneck, best chalk_ev players couldn't enter the pool. (2) chalk_max_stars 2→1 — only 2/6 winning drafts even had 1 star; at most 1 low-boost star allowed now. (3) chalk_star_boost_threshold 0.6→0.8 — Bam (0.9x) and Reaves (0.8x) weren't counted as stars; now they are, further limiting star-heavy lineups. (4) leverage_top_slots 1→2 — force 2 contrarian (boost≥1.5) players into 2.0x and 1.8x slots; winners consistently put high-boost players in top slots. (5) leverage_boost_threshold 1.0→1.5 — raise bar for what counts as contrarian in top slots. (6) All 4 new params externalized to model-config.json for Ben tuning.
